intro: The clearest split between Tink and Persona is how they charge: Tink uses subscription pricing, while Persona uses usage-based pricing at Pay per successful verification. Tink is European open banking platform providing bank account aggregation, payment initiation, and income verification APIs; it fits best for Fintech, Marketplace. Persona is Modular identity verification platform combining KYC, KYB, document checks, and custom rule-based decisioning; it fits best for Fintech, Marketplace. Pick Tink for Fintech, Marketplace; pick Persona for Fintech, Marketplace. Tink is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements. Persona is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements. Honest trade-offs: Tink is weak for consumer apps without regulatory requirements, and Persona is weak for consumer apps without regulatory requirements — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the KYC / KYB / AML API umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate.
quick_take: Tink is for fintech, marketplace; Persona is for fintech, marketplace; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Tink if you want subscription pricing and your workload matches fintech, marketplace.
verdict_b: Choose Persona if you want usage-based pricing at Pay per successful verification and your workload matches fintech, marketplace.

faq.a: A direct comparison depends on your usage profile and which pricing model fits better. Tink uses subscription pricing with no free tier published. Persona uses usage-based pricing at Pay per successful verification.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Tink to Persona?
faq.a: Migration is workable but not trivial, since both sit around medium lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Both score 4/5 on developer experience in our data. Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is Persona a good alternative to Tink?
faq.a: Persona sits in the same category as Tink, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. Persona is best fit for fintech, marketplace. Note: Persona is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements.
